input.background: For the illustrative data, we have established \(\bar{x}=603.64, \bar{y}=20.55, s_X=378.451, s_Y=11.725\) and \(r=0.737\).
input.question: Establish the regression model \(\hat{y}\).

X.description: Original predictor variable whose sample mean is x̄ = 603.64 and sample standard deviation is s_X = 378.451.

Y.description: Response variable to be predicted; its sample mean is ȳ = 20.55 and sample standard deviation is s_Y = 11.725.

x_bar.description: Sample mean of X used to compute the intercept of the regression line.

y_bar.description: Sample mean of Y used to compute the intercept of the regression line.

s_X.description: Sample standard deviation of X used to compute the slope of the regression line.

s_Y.description: Sample standard deviation of Y used to compute the slope of the regression line.

r.description: Sample correlation coefficient between X and Y used to compute the slope of the regression line.

y_hat.description: Fitted value of Y from the simple linear regression model ŷ = a + bX.

regression_slope_b.description: Slope of the regression line, computed as b = r * (s_Y / s_X).

regression_intercept_a.description: Intercept of the regression line, computed as a = ȳ − b * x̄.

answer.value: Use the given summary statistics to construct the simple linear regression model of Y on X. First compute the slope b = r * (s_Y / s_X) using r = 0.737, s_Y = 11.725, and s_X = 378.451. Then compute the intercept a = ȳ − b * x̄ using x̄ = 603.64 and ȳ = 20.55. Finally, write the regression model as ŷ = a + bX.
answer.description: Step-by-step procedure to establish the regression model ŷ using the provided summary statistics.
